title: Bench Press: Barbell
primer: The barbell bench press is a fundamental compound exercise for building chest mass and strength.
steps: Lie flat on bench with feet on the floor
Grip bar slightly wider than shoulder width
Unrack the bar and lower it to mid-chest
Press the bar back up to starting position
tips: Maintain a slight arch in your lower back
Don't bounce the bar off your chest

title: Bench Press: Dumbbell
primer: Dumbbell bench press allows greater range of motion and addresses muscle imbalances.
steps: Sit on bench with dumbbells on thighs
Lie back and press dumbbells to chest level
Press dumbbells up and slightly together
Lower with control to starting position
tips: Don't let dumbbells drift too wide

title: Bench Press: Incline Barbell
primer: The incline bench press emphasizes the upper chest and front deltoids.
steps: Grip bar slightly wider than shoulders

title: Bench Press: Incline Dumbbell
primer: Incline dumbbell press builds the upper chest with greater range of motion.

title: Bench Press: Decline Barbell
primer: Decline bench press targets the lower chest fibers.
tips: Don't go too steep of a decline

title: Chest Fly: Dumbbell
primer: Dumbbell flyes isolate the chest through a wide arc motion.
steps: Lie on bench holding dumbbells above chest
Lower arms out in a wide arc
Keep slight bend in elbows throughout
Return to start by squeezing chest
tips: Don't go lower than shoulder level

title: Chest Fly: Cable
primer: Cable flyes provide constant tension throughout the range of motion.

title: Chest Fly: Machine
primer: The pec deck machine isolates the chest in a safe, guided movement.
steps: Adjust seat so handles are at chest level
Grip handles with elbows slightly bent
Bring arms together in front of chest

title: Push-up
primer: The push-up is a classic bodyweight exercise that builds chest, shoulder and tricep strength.
steps: Start in plank position with hands shoulder-width apart
Keep body in straight line throughout

title: Push-up: Wide Grip
primer: Wide grip push-ups place greater emphasis on the chest.
steps: Place hands wider than shoulder width

title: Push-up: Diamond
primer: Diamond push-ups shift emphasis to the triceps and inner chest.
steps: Form diamond shape with hands under chest
tips: Difficult movement — start with knees if needed

title: Dip: Chest
primer: Chest dips are a powerful compound movement for the lower chest.
steps: Grip parallel bars and support bodyweight
Lower body until upper arm is parallel

title: Machine Chest Press
primer: The machine chest press provides a safe, stable press movement for chest development.
steps: Adjust seat height so handles are at chest level

title: Cable Chest Press
primer: Cable chest press provides constant tension and can be done at various angles.

title: Push-up: Incline
primer: Incline push-ups reduce bodyweight load and target the lower chest.

title: Svend Press
primer: The Svend press uses isometric chest contraction to build inner chest strength.
steps: Hold weight plate pressed between palms

title: Chest Dip: Machine
primer: Machine dips allow for consistent, assisted chest dips.

title: Low Cable Fly
primer: Low cable flyes target the upper chest with an upward arc motion.
steps: Stand centered and bring handles up and together

title: High Cable Fly
primer: High cable flyes target the lower chest with a downward arc motion.
steps: Stand centered and bring handles down and together

title: Landmine Press
primer: The landmine press works the chest and shoulders in a joint-friendly arc.
steps: Fix barbell end in corner or landmine attachment
Hold opposite end with both hands at chest

title: Push-up: Decline
primer: Decline push-ups target the upper chest by elevating the feet.

title: Push-up: Plyometric
primer: Plyometric push-ups build explosive upper body power.
steps: Explosively press up so hands leave floor

title: Bench Press: Close Grip
primer: Close grip bench press emphasizes the triceps while still working the chest.
steps: Grip bar just inside shoulder width
tips: Elbows should stay close to sides
Don't go too narrow — strains wrists

title: Dumbbell Pullover
primer: The dumbbell pullover works both the chest and lats simultaneously.
steps: Lie perpendicular on bench with shoulders on pad
Hold dumbbell with both hands above chest
Lower dumbbell behind head in arc

title: Chest Squeeze Press
primer: The squeeze press uses constant medial force to maximize inner chest activation.
steps: Lie on bench holding dumbbells together above chest
Press back up while maintaining squeeze

title: Pull-up
primer: Pull-ups are a foundational bodyweight exercise for back width and strength.
steps: Hang from bar with overhand grip wider than shoulders
Pull body up until chin clears bar
Lower with control to dead hang

title: Chin-up
primer: Chin-ups use an underhand grip which increases bicep involvement.
steps: Hang from bar with underhand shoulder-width grip
Pull up until chin over bar

title: Row: Barbell
primer: Barbell rows build back thickness and overall back strength.
steps: Hinge at hips holding barbell with overhand grip

title: Row: Dumbbell
primer: Single arm dumbbell rows allow for greater range of motion and address imbalances.
steps: Place one knee and hand on bench

title: Lat Pulldown: Machine
primer: Lat pulldowns are a great alternative to pull-ups for back width.
steps: Sit with knees secured under pad

title: Deadlift: Barbell
primer: The deadlift is one of the most effective full-body exercises for strength.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width, bar over mid-foot
Keep back straight, lift by extending hips and knees
Lower back to ground with control

title: Row: Cable Seated
primer: Seated cable rows build mid-back thickness with constant tension.
steps: Sit at cable station with feet on platform

title: Row: Machine
primer: Machine rows allow for strict form with chest support.
tips: Chest support removes lower back strain

title: Row: T-Bar
primer: T-bar rows are an effective barbell rowing variation for back thickness.
steps: Straddle bar with feet on platform if available
Hinge at hips and grip the bar

title: Pulldown: Close Grip
primer: Close grip pulldowns emphasize the lower lats.
steps: Use close grip or V-bar attachment

title: Row: Pendlay
primer: Pendlay rows use a strict dead-stop technique for greater back activation.
steps: Bar starts on ground each rep

title: Pull-up: Weighted
primer: Weighted pull-ups add resistance to the bodyweight pull-up for progressive overload.
steps: Hang from bar with overhand grip
Pull up until chin clears bar

title: Straight Arm Pulldown
primer: Straight arm pulldowns isolate the lats through a sweeping motion.
steps: Stand at cable with rope or bar attachment
Push handle down from overhead to hips

title: Rack Pull
primer: Rack pulls work the upper portion of the deadlift, targeting the upper back and traps.
steps: Set bar in rack at knee height or above
Grip bar and assume deadlift stance
Pull bar up to full extension

title: Face Pull
primer: Face pulls target the rear deltoids and external rotators for shoulder health.
steps: Set cable at head height with rope attachment
Pull rope to face with elbows high
tips: Great for posture and shoulder health

title: Hyperextension
primer: Hyperextensions target the erector spinae and lower back.
steps: Position on hyperextension bench with hips at pad edge
tips: Can hold weight for added resistance

title: Inverted Row
primer: Inverted rows are a bodyweight horizontal pull for back development.
steps: Set bar at waist height in rack
Hang underneath bar with overhand grip
tips: Keep body straight like a plank

title: Deadlift: Sumo
primer: Sumo deadlifts use a wide stance that shifts emphasis to the hips and inner thighs.
steps: Stand with feet wide, toes pointed out
Push knees out as you lift

title: Good Morning
primer: Good mornings strengthen the posterior chain through a hip hinge movement.
steps: Place bar on upper back like squat
Hinge at hips with slight knee bend
Lower torso until parallel to floor
Return to upright by driving hips forward

title: Pull-up: Neutral Grip
primer: Neutral grip pull-ups are a joint-friendly variation that targets the brachialis.
steps: Grip handles with palms facing each other
Pull up until chin over handles
Lower with control to dead hang
tips: Great for those with joint issues

title: Row: Meadows
primer: The Meadows row is a landmine variation that produces a unique back contraction.
steps: Stand beside end of barbell in landmine
Row barbell with outside hand to hip
tips: Rotate torso slightly for full stretch

title: Pullover: Cable
primer: Cable pullovers provide constant tension for lat development.
steps: Set cable at top with rope
Kneel facing away or bend at waist facing cable
Pull rope down in arc to hips

title: Deadlift: Trap Bar
primer: Trap bar deadlifts allow for a more upright torso and reduced spinal load.
steps: Step inside trap bar with feet hip-width
Grip handles and lift with upright torso

title: Lat Pulldown: Single Arm
primer: Single arm pulldowns address imbalances and allow for a longer range of motion.
steps: Grip handle overhead and pull to shoulder

title: Row: Seal
primer: Seal rows use a chest-supported position to isolate the back muscles.

title: Overhead Press: Barbell
primer: The barbell overhead press builds overall shoulder strength and mass.
steps: Clean or take bar from rack at shoulder height

title: Overhead Press: Dumbbell
primer: Dumbbell overhead press allows independent arm movement and greater range of motion.
tips: Neutral grip option for shoulder issues

title: Lateral Raise: Dumbbell
primer: Lateral raises directly target the medial deltoid for shoulder width.
steps: Raise arms out to sides to shoulder height

title: Lateral Raise: Cable
primer: Cable lateral raises provide constant tension throughout the movement.
steps: Raise arm out to shoulder height

title: Lateral Raise: Machine
primer: Machine lateral raises allow consistent form and easy load adjustment.

title: Front Raise: Dumbbell
primer: Front raises target the anterior deltoid directly.
steps: Stand holding dumbbells in front of thighs
Raise one or both arms to shoulder height

title: Front Raise: Barbell
primer: Barbell front raises work both anterior deltoids simultaneously.
tips: Use lighter weight than you think

title: Rear Delt Fly: Dumbbell
primer: Rear delt flyes isolate the posterior deltoid for balanced shoulder development.
steps: Hinge forward with slight knee bend

title: Rear Delt Fly: Machine
primer: Machine rear delt flyes isolate posterior deltoids with guided movement.
steps: Grip handles with arms extended forward

title: Rear Delt Fly: Cable
primer: Cable rear delt flyes provide constant tension on the posterior deltoid.

title: Arnold Press
primer: The Arnold press incorporates rotation for greater front and side delt activation.
steps: Start with palms facing you at shoulder height
Rotate palms outward as you press up

title: Upright Row: Barbell
primer: Upright rows work both the traps and deltoids.
steps: Hold barbell with close overhand grip
Pull bar vertically to chin height
tips: Wide grip reduces shoulder impingement risk

title: Upright Row: Dumbbell
primer: Dumbbell upright rows allow independent arm movement.
steps: Hold dumbbells in front of thighs
Pull up to chin keeping elbows high

title: Overhead Press: Machine
primer: Machine overhead press provides guided movement for safe shoulder pressing.
steps: Adjust seat to position handles at shoulder level

title: Push Press
primer: Push press uses leg drive to allow heavier overhead loads.
steps: Drive with legs and press bar overhead

title: Seated Dumbbell Press
primer: Seated dumbbell press allows strict form with back support.
steps: Sit on bench with back support
tips: Back support reduces lower back involvement

title: Cable Lateral Raise: Cross Body
primer: Cross-body cable raises target the medial deltoid from a different angle.
steps: Stand with cable on opposite side
tips: Different stretch than standard lateral raise

title: Band Pull Apart
primer: Band pull aparts strengthen the rear delts and improve shoulder posture.
steps: Hold band with arms extended forward
Pull band apart until fully stretched

title: Shoulder Press: Smith Machine
primer: Smith machine shoulder press provides a guided bar path for overhead pressing.
steps: Sit or stand under smith machine bar

title: Dumbbell Scaption
primer: Scaption raises work the shoulder in its natural plane for joint health.
steps: Hold dumbbells at sides with thumbs up
Raise arms to about 30 degrees forward

title: Lateral Raise: Behind Back Cable
primer: Behind-back cable lateral raises provide a unique tension arc for the medial delt.
tips: Different peak tension than standard lateral

title: Seated Bent-Over Rear Delt Raise
primer: Seated bent-over raises isolate the rear deltoid with reduced body swing.
steps: Sit at edge of bench bent forward over thighs

title: Overhead Press: Kettlebell
primer: Kettlebell overhead press develops shoulder stability due to the offset load.
tips: Can be done bilaterally or unilaterally

title: Bicep Curl: Barbell
primer: Barbell curls allow maximum load for bicep development.
steps: Stand holding barbell with shoulder-width supinated grip
Curl bar up to shoulder height

title: Bicep Curl: Dumbbell
primer: Dumbbell curls allow individual arm work and supination for full bicep contraction.
steps: Hold dumbbells at sides with palms forward
Curl weights up while keeping elbows stationary

title: Bicep Curl: EZ Bar
primer: EZ bar curls reduce wrist strain while still effectively working the biceps.
steps: Hold EZ bar with angled grip
Curl bar up to chin level
tips: Easier on wrists than straight bar

title: Hammer Curl
primer: Hammer curls target the brachialis and brachioradialis for arm thickness.
steps: Hold dumbbells with neutral grip (thumbs up)
Curl up while maintaining neutral grip
tips: Great for forearm and brachialis development

title: Preacher Curl: Barbell
primer: Preacher curls use the preacher bench to isolate the biceps and eliminate cheating.
steps: Sit at preacher bench with upper arms on pad
tips: Don't let elbows lift off pad

title: Preacher Curl: Dumbbell
primer: Single arm preacher curls allow for concentrated bicep work.
steps: Sit at preacher bench with one upper arm on pad
tips: Feel the stretch at the bottom

title: Concentration Curl
primer: Concentration curls isolate one bicep at a time for focused development.
steps: Sit on bench with elbow on inner thigh

title: Cable Curl
primer: Cable curls provide constant tension throughout the curl movement.
steps: Stand at cable station with bar at low position

title: Incline Dumbbell Curl
primer: Incline curls put the biceps in a stretched position for greater activation.
steps: Sit on incline bench with arms hanging

title: Drag Curl
primer: Drag curls move the elbows back as you curl, creating unique bicep peak contraction.
steps: Hold barbell and drag it up body
Elbows move behind torso as bar rises
tips: Different peak contraction than standard curl

title: Cable Hammer Curl
primer: Cable hammer curls use a rope attachment to work the brachialis with constant tension.
steps: Curl up while maintaining neutral grip

title: Reverse Curl
primer: Reverse curls target the brachioradialis and forearm extensors.
steps: Curl bar up to shoulder height
tips: Use lighter weight than standard curl

title: Zottman Curl
primer: Zottman curls combine a supinated curl with a pronated lowering phase.
steps: Rotate to pronated grip at top
tips: Works both flexion muscles and extensors

title: Bayesian Curl
primer: Bayesian curls position the elbow behind the body to increase bicep stretch.
steps: Stand with arm extended back gripping cable
tips: Great for long head of bicep

title: Bicep Curl: Spider
primer: Spider curls use a prone position to isolate the biceps completely.
steps: Lie prone on incline bench with arms hanging
Curl dumbbells up to shoulder level

title: Bicep Curl: Cross Body Cable
primer: Cross-body cable curls provide tension from a different angle for inner bicep development.
steps: Set cable to side at low height
Curl across body toward opposite shoulder

title: Overhead Cable Curl
primer: Overhead cable curls target the long head of the bicep with the arm raised.
steps: Set cables high on both sides
Stand between cables with arms extended

title: Tricep Pushdown: Cable
primer: Cable pushdowns isolate the triceps through a downward pressing motion.
steps: Set cable high with bar or rope attachment

title: Tricep Pushdown: Rope
primer: Rope pushdowns allow for a wider elbow flare at the bottom for greater tricep contraction.
steps: Push down and spread rope at bottom
tips: Spread rope at bottom for extra contraction

title: Skull Crusher: Barbell
primer: Skull crushers are a classic mass-building movement for the triceps.
steps: Lie on bench holding bar with narrow grip
Lower bar toward forehead by bending elbows
tips: Can use EZ bar for wrist comfort

title: Skull Crusher: Dumbbell
primer: Dumbbell skull crushers allow independent arm movement for tricep development.
steps: Lie on bench with dumbbells over chest
Lower dumbbells toward temples by bending elbows

title: Tricep Extension: Overhead Cable
primer: Overhead cable extensions stretch the long head of the tricep for maximum activation.
steps: Set cable low with rope attachment

title: Tricep Extension: Overhead Dumbbell
primer: Overhead dumbbell extensions target the long head of the tricep.
steps: Hold dumbbell overhead with both hands
Lower weight behind head by bending elbows

title: Dip: Tricep
primer: Tricep dips are a bodyweight exercise that directly targets the triceps.
steps: Grip parallel bars with torso upright
tips: Lean forward for more chest involvement

title: Kickback: Tricep
primer: Tricep kickbacks isolate the triceps through extension behind the body.
steps: Pin upper arm parallel to floor
Extend arm back to full extension
tips: Upper arm must stay parallel to floor

title: Tricep Extension: Single Arm Cable
primer: Single arm cable pushdowns address tricep imbalances.
steps: Set cable high with single handle
Grip handle with one hand at chest level

title: Close Grip Push-up
primer: Close grip push-ups shift the work to the triceps.
steps: Press back up keeping elbows close to sides

title: Tricep Extension: Band
primer: Band tricep extensions provide accommodating resistance for tricep development.
steps: Extend arms downward to full extension
tips: Various anchor heights change the angle

title: JM Press
primer: The JM press is a hybrid between skull crushers and close grip bench press.
steps: Lie on bench with close grip on bar
tips: Hybrid movement for triceps and chest

title: Tate Press
primer: Tate press uses a pronated grip to target the triceps through a unique range of motion.
steps: Lie on bench with dumbbells over chest
Elbows flared out, dumbbells near chest

title: Wrist Curl: Barbell
primer: Wrist curls directly target the wrist flexors in the forearm.
steps: Sit with forearms on bench, wrists over edge

title: Wrist Curl: Reverse
primer: Reverse wrist curls target the wrist extensors for balanced forearm development.
steps: Sit with forearms on bench, wrists over edge

title: Farmer Carry
primer: Farmer carries build grip strength, core stability and total body conditioning.
steps: Pick up heavy dumbbells or kettlebells
Walk for set distance or time

title: Plate Pinch
primer: Plate pinching develops pinch grip strength essential for many pulling movements.
steps: Grip weight plate between thumb and fingers
tips: Progress by increasing time or weight

title: Wrist Roller
primer: The wrist roller builds forearm strength through a rolling winding motion.
steps: Hold roller at shoulder height with arms extended
tips: Arms extended is harder than bent
Both winding and unwinding are working reps

title: Suitcase Carry
primer: Suitcase carries challenge lateral core stability while building grip strength.
steps: Pick up weight on one side only
Walk for set distance keeping torso upright
tips: Core works hard to stay upright

title: Dead Hang
primer: Dead hangs build grip endurance and decompress the spine.
steps: Hang from bar with arms fully extended

title: Towel Pull-up
primer: Towel pull-ups challenge grip strength through an unstable circular grip.

title: Squat: Barbell
primer: The barbell squat is the king of lower body exercises for strength and mass.
steps: Lower by bending hips and knees

title: Squat: Front
primer: Front squats place the bar in front shifting emphasis to the quads and upper back.
tips: High elbows prevent bar from rolling
Greater quad activation than back squat

title: Squat: Goblet
primer: Goblet squats use a front-loaded position to teach squat mechanics.
steps: Hold weight at chest in goblet position
Squat down with elbows inside knees

title: Squat: Bulgarian Split
primer: Bulgarian split squats challenge single-leg strength and hip mobility.
steps: Drive through front heel to stand

title: Leg Press: Machine
primer: The leg press develops quadriceps without spinal loading.
steps: Sit in machine with feet on platform
Press through heels to extend legs

title: Leg Extension: Machine
primer: Leg extensions isolate the quadriceps through knee extension.
steps: Sit in machine with pad against shins
tips: Full extension for peak quad contraction

title: Lunge: Barbell
primer: Barbell lunges add load to the lunge pattern for increased strength demand.
steps: Push through front foot to return

title: Lunge: Dumbbell
primer: Dumbbell lunges develop leg strength, balance and coordination.
steps: Step forward and lower into lunge
Push through front foot to return

title: Step-up: Dumbbell
primer: Step-ups build unilateral leg strength and can improve knee tracking.
steps: Step up onto box or bench with one foot
tips: Drive through the heel on box

title: Hack Squat: Machine
primer: The hack squat machine allows deep squatting with supported back position.
steps: Position shoulders under pads with feet on platform
tips: High foot position targets glutes more

title: Squat: Sumo
primer: Sumo squats use a wider stance to target the inner thighs and glutes.
steps: Stand with feet wider than shoulder width, toes pointed out
Squat down keeping knees tracking over toes
tips: Can hold dumbbell for goblet variation

title: Sissy Squat
primer: Sissy squats are an advanced bodyweight quad isolation exercise.
steps: Stand with feet together, hold for balance
Lean back while raising on toes
Bend knees and lower torso backward
tips: Challenging for knees — build up gradually

title: Wall Sit
primer: Wall sits build quad endurance through isometric muscle contraction.
steps: Slide down until thighs are parallel to floor

title: Leg Press: Narrow Stance
primer: Narrow stance leg press places more emphasis on the outer quad.
steps: Place feet close together and high on platform

title: Split Squat
primer: Split squats build single-leg strength in a stable position.
steps: Take a large step forward into lunge position
Push through front foot to stand

title: Box Jump
primer: Box jumps develop explosive lower body power.
tips: Don't jump down — step down to protect knees

title: Pistol Squat
primer: Pistol squats are an advanced single-leg squat requiring strength, balance and mobility.
steps: Stand on one leg with other leg extended forward
tips: Very challenging — use support to learn

title: Squat: Smith Machine
primer: Smith machine squats provide a guided bar path for squatting safely.
steps: Set bar on upper back under smith machine
tips: Feet can be placed further forward
Fixed bar path changes mechanics slightly

title: Lunge: Reverse
primer: Reverse lunges reduce knee stress compared to forward lunges.
steps: Push through front foot to return
tips: Easier on knees than forward lunge

title: Romanian Deadlift: Barbell
primer: Romanian deadlifts emphasize the hamstrings through a hip hinge.
steps: Hold bar at hip level with slight knee bend
Hinge at hips, lowering bar along legs

title: Romanian Deadlift: Dumbbell
primer: Dumbbell RDLs allow for a natural bar path and individual arm freedom.
steps: Hold dumbbells in front of thighs

title: Leg Curl: Machine
primer: Leg curls isolate the hamstrings through knee flexion.
steps: Lie prone on leg curl machine
Curl legs up to full flexion

title: Leg Curl: Seated
primer: Seated leg curls work the hamstrings with a different muscle length than lying curls.
steps: Sit in machine with pad against lower legs

title: Nordic Hamstring Curl
primer: Nordic curls are an advanced eccentric hamstring exercise for injury prevention.
steps: Lower body toward floor by extending knees
Use arms to push back up
tips: Eccentric focus is the main benefit

title: Leg Curl: Dumbbell
primer: Dumbbell leg curls can be done on a bench as an alternative to machine curls.
steps: Lie prone on bench with dumbbell between feet
Curl dumbbell up by bending knees

title: Glute Ham Raise
primer: The glute ham raise works both the hamstrings and glutes through hip extension and knee flexion.
steps: Position in GHR machine with feet anchored
tips: Works hip extension and knee flexion

title: Stiff Leg Deadlift
primer: Stiff leg deadlifts stretch and work the hamstrings with less knee bend than RDL.
tips: Slight knee bend to protect joint

title: Cable Pull Through
primer: Cable pull throughs target the posterior chain through a hip hinge movement.
steps: Stand in front of cable with rope between legs
Hinge at hips while reaching back through legs
Drive hips forward to return to standing
tips: Drive with hips not lower back

title: Good Morning: Banded
primer: Banded good mornings build posterior chain strength safely.
steps: Place band behind neck and under feet
Hinge at hips with slight knee bend

title: Hip Thrust: Barbell
primer: Barbell hip thrusts are among the most effective exercises for glute development.
steps: Sit against bench with barbell across hips
Drive through heels to thrust hips up

title: Hip Thrust: Dumbbell
primer: Dumbbell hip thrusts offer a more accessible hip thrust variation.
steps: Sit against bench with dumbbell on hips
Drive through heels to thrust hips up

title: Glute Bridge
primer: Glute bridges build glute strength from a supine position.
steps: Lie on back with knees bent, feet flat on floor
Drive through heels to lift hips

title: Glute Kickback: Cable
primer: Cable kickbacks isolate the glutes through hip extension.
steps: Attach ankle strap to cable at low position
Stand and kick leg back and up

title: Hip Abduction: Machine
primer: Hip abduction machine works the glute medius for hip stability.
steps: Sit in machine with pads on outer thighs

title: Donkey Kick
primer: Donkey kicks isolate the glutes through hip extension on all fours.
steps: Kick one leg back and up

title: Fire Hydrant
primer: Fire hydrants target the glute medius through lateral hip abduction.
steps: Raise one knee out to the side

title: Sumo Deadlift: High Pull
primer: The sumo deadlift high pull combines posterior chain and upper body work.
steps: Pull explosively into high pull position

title: Cable Hip Abduction
primer: Cable hip abduction works the glute medius with constant tension.
steps: Attach ankle strap to low cable
Stand and lift leg out to side

title: Frog Pump
primer: Frog pumps intensely target the glutes through a unique foot-together bridge.
steps: Lie on back with feet together and knees out
Drive hips up by squeezing glutes

title: Hip Thrust: Single Leg
primer: Single leg hip thrusts increase the demand on each glute individually.
steps: Sit against bench with one leg extended
Drive through planted foot to thrust hips up

title: Resistance Band Hip Thrust
primer: Banded hip thrusts add accommodating resistance to glute development.
steps: Loop band over hips and anchor under feet
Band provides more resistance at lockout

title: Calf Raise: Standing
primer: Standing calf raises develop the gastrocnemius through plantar flexion.
steps: Stand on calf raise platform with weight on shoulders
tips: Full range of motion is essential

title: Calf Raise: Seated
primer: Seated calf raises target the soleus with a bent-knee position.
steps: Sit at seated calf raise machine
Place weight pad on lower thighs
Lower heels and raise on toes

title: Calf Raise: Donkey
primer: Donkey calf raises allow for exceptional range of motion and stretch.
steps: Bend at waist placing forearms on support
Partner or weight sits on hips
Lower heels and raise onto toes

title: Calf Raise: Single Leg
primer: Single leg calf raises double the load on each calf.
steps: Stand on one leg on edge of step
tips: Use bodyweight alone for many reps

title: Calf Raise: Leg Press
primer: Calf raises on the leg press provide a safe way to load the calves.
steps: Use leg press machine with weight loaded
Sit with balls of feet at bottom of platform
Extend and flex ankles through full range
tips: High reps work well for calves

title: Jump Rope
primer: Jump rope develops calf endurance and cardiovascular fitness.
steps: Hold rope handles at hip height
Jump with both feet as rope passes

title: Calf Raise: Smith Machine
primer: Smith machine calf raises provide stability for heavy calf training.
steps: Position bar on upper back under smith machine
Raise and lower on toes through full range
tips: Use platform for better range of motion

title: Tibialis Raise
primer: Tibialis raises strengthen the anterior tibialis for calf balance and shin splint prevention.
steps: Lean against wall with heels on floor
Or use a tib bar with added weight

title: Crunch
primer: Crunches are a fundamental abdominal exercise targeting the rectus abdominis.
steps: Lie on back with knees bent
Lift shoulders off ground using abs

title: Sit-up
primer: Sit-ups work the abdominals and hip flexors through a larger range of motion.
steps: Lie on back with knees bent
tips: Can anchor feet for more hip flexor involvement

title: Plank
primer: The plank builds core stability and endurance through an isometric hold.
steps: Keep body straight from head to heels

title: Side Plank
primer: Side planks target the obliques and lateral core stabilizers.
steps: Lie on side and prop on forearm
Raise hips so body is straight
tips: Can add hip dips for dynamic variation

title: Leg Raise: Hanging
primer: Hanging leg raises target the lower abdominals and hip flexors.
steps: Raise legs to horizontal or higher

title: Leg Raise: Lying
primer: Lying leg raises target the lower abdominals.
steps: Keep legs straight or slightly bent
Lower with control without touching floor

title: Ab Wheel Rollout
primer: Ab wheel rollouts are one of the most demanding core exercises.
steps: Kneel with hands on ab wheel
Roll forward as far as possible

title: Cable Crunch
primer: Cable crunches allow for heavy weighted abdominal training.
steps: Kneel at cable with rope attachment
tips: Keep hips stationary — bend from waist

title: Russian Twist
primer: Russian twists target the obliques through rotational movement.
steps: Sit on floor with knees bent
Can hold weight for added resistance
tips: Feet can be raised for more challenge

title: Bicycle Crunch
primer: Bicycle crunches work both the rectus abdominis and obliques simultaneously.
steps: Lie on back with hands behind head
Alternate bringing elbow to opposite knee
Extend opposite leg as you rotate
tips: Slow and controlled is more effective

title: Dragon Flag
primer: Dragon flags are an advanced core exercise requiring extreme abdominal strength.
steps: Lie on bench, grip behind head
Raise body to vertical supported on shoulder blades
Lower body as a rigid unit
tips: One of the hardest core exercises

title: V-up
primer: V-ups simultaneously work upper and lower abdominals.

title: Hollow Body Hold
primer: The hollow body hold is a fundamental gymnastics core exercise.
steps: Raise arms overhead and legs slightly off floor
tips: Lower back must stay on floor
Arms and legs create leverage challenge

title: Toe Touch Crunch
primer: Toe touch crunches target the upper abdominals with legs vertical.
steps: Lie on back with legs raised vertical
Crunch up reaching hands toward feet
tips: Short range of motion is fine

title: Mountain Climber
primer: Mountain climbers combine core stability with cardiovascular conditioning.
steps: Rapidly alternate driving knees to chest

title: Pallof Press
primer: The Pallof press develops anti-rotational core stability.
steps: Stand sideways to cable with handle at chest

title: Flutter Kick
primer: Flutter kicks build lower abdominal endurance.
steps: Lie on back with legs elevated
Alternate raising and lowering legs in small range
Keep lower back pressed to floor
tips: Can do for time or reps

title: Windshield Wiper
primer: Windshield wipers target the obliques through rotational leg movement.
steps: Hang from bar with legs raised to horizontal

title: Oblique Crunch
primer: Oblique crunches target the side abdominals.
steps: Lie on back with knees dropped to one side

title: Wood Chop: Cable
primer: Cable wood chops build rotational power and oblique strength.
steps: Set cable high on one side
Pull cable diagonally down and across body

title: Shrug: Barbell
primer: Barbell shrugs directly target the trapezius for neck and upper back thickness.
steps: Hold barbell in front with arms extended

title: Shrug: Dumbbell
primer: Dumbbell shrugs allow for a slightly different movement arc.

title: Shrug: Cable
primer: Cable shrugs provide constant tension on the trapezius.
tips: Can be done from front or behind

title: Farmer Carry: Trap Bar
primer: Trap bar carries allow for heavier loads with improved balance.
steps: Load trap bar and pick up from center

title: Power Shrug
primer: Power shrugs use a slight leg drive to move heavier trap loads.
steps: Slight knee bend and drive with legs
Shrug explosively at top of leg extension
tips: Good for building trap mass quickly

title: Behind Back Shrug
primer: Behind-back shrugs target the middle traps from a retracted position.

title: Overhead Carry
primer: Overhead carries challenge shoulder stability and trap strength.
steps: Press weight overhead to full extension
tips: Wrist stacked over elbow over shoulder
Core works hard to resist lean

title: Neck Extension
primer: Neck extensions strengthen the muscles of the posterior neck.
steps: On hands and knees or face down
Hold plate on back of head

title: Power Clean
primer: The power clean is a full-body Olympic-style movement for explosive power.
steps: Start with bar at mid-shin in deadlift position
Pull bar explosively and extend hips
Drop under bar to front rack position

title: Clean and Press
primer: Clean and press combines the clean with an overhead press for full-body development.
steps: Clean bar to front rack position
tips: Can strict press or push press

title: Kettlebell Swing
primer: Kettlebell swings develop explosive hip power and posterior chain strength.
steps: Stand over kettlebell with feet shoulder-width
Hinge and swing kettlebell back between legs
Let kettlebell float to shoulder height
tips: Let the weight float — don't pull with arms

title: Thruster
primer: Thrusters combine a front squat with an overhead press in one fluid movement.
steps: Hold bar in front rack position
Use leg drive to stand and press overhead
tips: Use leg drive to power the press
One of the most demanding exercises

title: Burpee
primer: Burpees are a total body conditioning exercise that combines multiple movements.
steps: Stand, then drop hands to floor
Explosively stand and jump with arms overhead

title: Turkish Get-up
primer: The Turkish get-up is a full-body movement that develops strength, stability and coordination.
steps: Lie on floor with weight pressed overhead
Rise from floor to standing while keeping weight overhead
Reverse the movement back to floor
tips: Very technical — learn without weight first

title: Snatch: Barbell
primer: The snatch is an Olympic lift requiring full body power and technical proficiency.
steps: Wide grip on barbell at floor
Punch arms overhead and drop under bar
tips: Build up slowly with PVC pipe first

title: Kettlebell Clean
primer: The kettlebell clean delivers the bell to rack position efficiently.
steps: Drive hips and pull kettlebell to rack position
tips: Bell should not bang the forearm

title: Kettlebell Snatch
primer: The kettlebell snatch is a ballistic full body exercise.
steps: Pull and punch overhead in one motion
tips: Punch through at top to avoid wrist bang

title: Dumbbell Complex
primer: Dumbbell complexes string together multiple exercises for metabolic conditioning.
steps: Perform set of each without putting dumbbells down
Complete all exercises as one complex
tips: Common exercises: RDL, row, clean, press, squat

title: Medicine Ball Slam
primer: Medicine ball slams build explosive total body power and core strength.
steps: Catch on bounce or pick up
tips: Use a non-bounce med ball on hard surfaces

title: Battle Ropes
primer: Battle ropes develop upper body power and cardiovascular endurance.
steps: Create waves by alternating or simultaneous arm movements

title: Rope Climb
primer: Rope climbing builds upper body pulling strength and grip endurance.
steps: Grip rope with both hands above head
Use arms and legs to climb upward
tips: Wrap rope around foot for leg assist

title: Sled Push
primer: Sled pushes build leg drive and cardiovascular fitness.
steps: Grip handles and drive with legs
tips: Drive through the balls of your feet

title: Sled Pull
primer: Sled pulls build posterior chain and back strength through backwards walking.

title: Tire Flip
primer: Tire flips develop explosive lower body and upper body power.
steps: Grip under tire with both hands

title: Atlas Stone
primer: Atlas stone training develops full body strength and grip.
steps: Grip stone and load onto thighs

title: Bench Press: Pause
primer: Paused bench press eliminates the stretch reflex for greater strength development.

title: Squat: Pause
primer: Paused squats develop strength out of the hole and improve technique.
steps: Hold the bottom position for 2-3 seconds
tips: Keeps tension in the bottom position

title: Deadlift: Romanian Single Leg
primer: Single leg RDLs challenge balance, hip stability and unilateral hamstring strength.
steps: Hinge forward lowering torso and raising back leg

title: Hip Circle
primer: Hip circles improve hip mobility and warm up the hip complex.

title: Cossack Squat
primer: Cossack squats improve hip mobility and lateral strength.
steps: Wide stance with toes pointed out
Shift weight to one side and squat on that leg

title: Adductor Machine
primer: The adductor machine targets the inner thigh muscles.
steps: Sit in machine with legs against outer pads

title: Copenhagen Plank
primer: Copenhagen planks strengthen the adductors and lateral core.
steps: Side plank position with top foot on bench
Raise bottom leg to meet top leg

title: Glute Squeeze: Band
primer: Banded glute exercises activate the glutes before training.
tips: Great for activation before lower body training
Focus on feeling the glutes work

title: Hack Squat: Barbell
primer: The barbell hack squat is a free-weight squat variation holding the bar behind the legs.
steps: Squat down keeping bar behind legs

title: Jefferson Squat
primer: The Jefferson squat is an asymmetrical movement that trains the legs and core.

title: Zercher Squat
primer: Zercher squats hold the bar in the crooks of the elbows for unique loading.

title: Sumo Squat: Barbell
primer: Sumo stance squats with barbell develop the inner thighs and glutes.
steps: Set feet wider than shoulder-width with toes pointed out

title: Snatch Grip Deadlift
primer: Snatch grip deadlifts use a wide grip for greater upper back and trap development.
steps: Use wide snatch-width grip on barbell
Deadlift as normal but from a lower position
tips: Very wide grip changes the mechanics
Lower back angle is more horizontal

title: Nordic Walk
primer: Various loaded carries develop functional strength and conditioning.

title: Kettlebell Goblet Squat

steps: Hold kettlebell at chest by horns
Squat down with elbows inside knees at bottom
tips: Excellent teaching tool for squat pattern

title: Lunge: Lateral
primer: Lateral lunges develop frontal plane leg strength and hip mobility.
steps: Squat on that leg keeping other leg straight

title: Lunge: Curtsy
primer: Curtsy lunges challenge the glutes and hip abductors through a cross-body step.
steps: Step one foot behind and across opposite leg

title: Hip Extension: Cable
primer: Cable hip extensions isolate the glutes and hamstrings through hip extension.

title: Leg Press: Single Leg
primer: Single leg press addresses muscle imbalances between legs.

title: Calf Raise: Standing Unilateral
primer: Unilateral calf raises double the workload on each calf.

title: Romanian Deadlift: Single Leg Cable
primer: Cable single leg RDLs provide constant tension while challenging balance.

title: Barbell Hip Thrust: Paused

title: Step-up: Barbell

title: Abduction: Standing Band
primer: Banded standing abductions target the glute medius for hip stability.
steps: Stand and raise one leg out to side

title: Barbell Rollout
primer: Barbell rollouts challenge the core through anti-extension.

title: Ab Crunch: Machine
primer: Ab crunch machines allow for weighted abdominal training.
steps: Sit in crunch machine with hands gripping handles

title: Squat Jump

steps: Explode upward jumping as high as possible

primer: Broad jumps develop horizontal explosive power.
steps: Jump forward as far as possible

primer: Depth jumps use the stretch-shortening cycle for maximum power development.

title: Sprint: Resisted

title: Landmine Squat

title: Step-up: Lateral

primer: Handstand holds develop shoulder stability and body awareness.

primer: Clap push-ups develop explosive upper body power.

title: Trap Bar Shrug
primer: Trap bar shrugs allow for neutral grip and heavier trap loading.

primer: Supinated cable rows increase bicep involvement alongside back muscles.

primer: External rotation exercises strengthen the rotator cuff for shoulder health.

primer: Trap 3 raises target the lower trapezius for shoulder health.

title: Band Curl
primer: Band curls provide accommodating resistance for bicep development.

steps: Lie on back with one knee bent
Drive through planted foot to bridge

title: Good Morning: Seated
primer: Seated good mornings isolate the hip hinge without leg drive.
steps: Sit on bench with bar on upper back

title: Hip Thrust: Kettlebell
primer: Kettlebell hip thrusts provide an accessible hip thrust option.
steps: Sit against bench with kettlebell on hips
Drive through heels to thrust hips

title: Power Clean: Hang
primer: Hang power cleans develop explosive hip extension from a standing start.
steps: Drop under bar to catch in front rack
tips: Starting from hip removes the floor pull

title: Snatch: Hang
primer: Hang snatches develop explosive pulling power from a hanging position.
steps: Hold bar at hip level with wide grip

title: Zercher Carry
primer: Zercher carries build core strength and stability with unusual loading.

title: Pull-apart: Band Overhead
primer: Overhead band pull aparts target the lower trapezius and improve overhead mobility.
steps: Hold band overhead with wide grip
Pull band apart while keeping arms overhead
tips: Works lower trap in lengthened position

title: Tibialis Curl
primer: Tibialis curls isolate the anterior tibialis for shin development.

title: Bicep Curl: Machine
primer: Machine bicep curls provide guided movement for isolated bicep development.

title: Hip Abductor: Cable Walk
primer: Cable hip abductor walks strengthen the glute medius for walking mechanics.
tips: Targets hip abductors through dynamic movement

title: Trap Raise: Cable
primer: Cable trap raises target the upper trapezius through direct shrugging.
steps: Hold cable at sides at arms length

title: Chest Press: Alternating Dumbbell
primer: Alternating dumbbell presses increase core rotation and challenge stability.
steps: Start with both dumbbells at chest
Press one arm at a time alternating
Other arm holds at chest or top

title: RDL: B-Stance
primer: B-stance RDLs are a semi-unilateral variation for targeting one leg at a time.
steps: Stand with one foot forward as main leg
Other foot toes touch floor behind for balance only
Hinge at hips focusing on front leg
tips: Most work done by front leg
More accessible than full single leg RDL

title: Nordic Hamstring Curl: Eccentric
primer: Eccentric Nordic curls build hamstring strength and reduce injury risk significantly.
steps: Slowly lower toward floor as slowly as possible
Push back up with hands if needed
tips: Eccentric is the main training stimulus
As slow as possible on the way down

title: Hip Thrust: American
primer: American hip thrusts add posterior pelvic tilt at the top for greater glute contraction.
steps: At top position, tilt pelvis posteriorly
Hold briefly at maximum glute contraction
tips: Posterior tilt at top adds contraction
Slightly different from standard hip thrust

title: Landmine Lateral Raise
primer: Landmine lateral raises provide a unique resistance curve for deltoid development.
steps: Hold end of barbell in landmine
tips: Different resistance curve than cables or dumbbells

title: Ring Face Pull
primer: Ring face pulls develop rear deltoids and external rotators using gymnastic rings.
steps: Pull rings to face with elbows high

title: Romanian Deadlift: Paused
primer: Paused RDLs increase hamstring time under tension at the stretch position.

title: Reverse Lunge: Barbell
primer: Barbell reverse lunges add load to the joint-friendly reverse lunge pattern.
steps: Push through front foot to return

title: Single Leg Calf Raise: Weighted
primer: Weighted single leg calf raises allow progressive overload for calf development.
steps: Hold dumbbell in one hand for extra load
Balance on one foot at edge of step
Lower heel and raise onto toes
tips: Full range of motion every rep

title: Barbell Complex
primer: Barbell complexes chain multiple exercises for metabolic and strength conditioning.
steps: Perform all reps of each exercise without setting bar down
Common: deadlift, hang clean, front squat, press
tips: Bar never touches floor within set
Great for conditioning and fat loss
Start light to learn the sequence

title: Bear Complex
primer: The Bear Complex is a challenging 5-exercise barbell sequence.
steps: Power clean + front squat + push press + back squat + behind neck press = 1 rep
tips: Each sequence counts as one rep
Bar cannot rest on floor within a set

title: Romanian Deadlift: Dumbbell Single Leg
primer: Single leg dumbbell RDLs challenge unilateral hamstring strength and hip stability.
steps: Balance on one leg holding dumbbell
Hinge forward lowering dumbbell toward floor
tips: More balance focused than barbell version

title: Lateral Raise: Bent Over Cable
primer: Bent-over cable raises target the rear deltoid through lateral movement.
steps: Bend at hips with cable below

title: Kneeling Cable Pull Down
primer: Kneeling cable pulldowns isolate the lats by removing lower body involvement.
steps: Pull handle from overhead to chest

title: Hip Flexor: Cable
primer: Cable hip flexor exercises strengthen the hip flexors for athletic performance.
steps: Attach ankle strap to low cable

title: Chest Dip: Weighted
primer: Weighted chest dips build significant chest and tricep mass.
steps: Grip dip bars and lean forward

title: Tricep Pushdown: V-Bar
primer: V-bar pushdowns target the triceps with a specific grip angle.

title: Dumbbell Fly: Cable Hybrid
primer: This hybrid movement combines dumbbell and cable resistance for unique chest training.
steps: Hold dumbbell in one hand, cable in other
tips: Both sides need to work together

title: Glute Bridge: Banded
primer: Banded glute bridges add lateral resistance to challenge hip abductors alongside glutes.
steps: Lie on back with knees bent
Drive through heels pushing knees out against band

title: Dumbbell Lateral Raise: Lying
primer: Lying lateral raises isolate one deltoid at a time with reduced body involvement.

title: Back Squat: High Bar
primer: High bar squats with bar on the traps encourage a more upright torso and quad development.
steps: Squat to depth keeping torso upright
tips: Upright torso with high bar position

title: Back Squat: Low Bar
primer: Low bar squats allow more hip drive and greater loads due to better leverages.
steps: Position bar lower on rear deltoids
Stand with shoulder-width or wider stance
tips: More forward lean than high bar

title: Spider Curl: Cable
primer: Cable spider curls use a prone position with cable for bicep isolation.
steps: Set cable low and lie prone on incline bench

title: Leg Extension: Single
primer: Single leg extensions address quad imbalances between legs.
steps: Extend one leg to full extension

title: Deadlift: Snatch Grip
primer: Snatch grip deadlifts use a very wide grip for greater upper back and trap development.
steps: Grip bar with very wide snatch grip
Deadlift from floor maintaining neutral spine
tips: Wide grip increases range of motion
Back must work harder to maintain position

title: Crab Reach
primer: Crab reaches improve thoracic mobility and shoulder flexibility.
steps: Start in crab position (hands and feet on floor)
Raise one arm and rotate chest toward ceiling

title: Cuban Rotation: Cable
primer: Cable Cuban rotations strengthen the shoulder external rotators.
steps: Rotate forearm up from horizontal to vertical

title: Box Step Down: Single Leg
primer: Single leg step downs develop eccentric quad strength and knee control.
steps: Stand on box with one leg
Slowly lower other heel toward floor
Keep knee tracking over toes throughout
tips: Knee should track over second toe

title: Prone Hip Extension
primer: Prone hip extensions target the glutes from a face-down position.
steps: Keep one leg straight and raise it

title: Landmine Press: Single Arm
primer: Single arm landmine presses develop shoulder stability through an arc of motion.
steps: Hold end of barbell at shoulder
tips: Great for those with shoulder issues

title: Tricep Extension: Overhead EZ Bar
primer: EZ bar overhead extensions target the long head of the triceps with reduced wrist strain.
steps: Hold EZ bar overhead with angled grip
Lower behind head by bending elbows
tips: Easier on wrists than straight bar

title: Barbell Curl: Slow Eccentric
primer: Slow eccentric curls maximize muscle damage and growth stimulus.

title: Single Leg Press: Hip Press
primer: Hip press machines target the glutes and hip extensors.
steps: Position in machine with feet on platform at hip height

title: Lying Dumbbell Row
primer: Lying dumbbell rows eliminate momentum for pure back isolation.

title: Russian Twist: Weighted
primer: Weighted Russian twists build oblique strength with progressive overload.
steps: Sit on floor leaning back with weight
Rotate to touch weight on each side of floor
tips: Weight held at chest increases challenge

title: Copenhagen Adductor
primer: Copenhagen adductor exercises strengthen the inner thigh for injury prevention.
steps: Lie on side with top foot on bench
Raise bottom leg to meet top leg

title: Banded Push-up
primer: Banded push-ups add accommodating resistance that increases at the lockout.
steps: Loop band around back and through hands

title: Wrist Curl: Dumbbell
primer: Dumbbell wrist curls allow independent forearm training.
steps: Sit with forearm on thigh, wrist over knee

title: Landmine Rotation: Anti
primer: Anti-rotation landmine exercises build rotational core stability.
steps: Hold end of barbell with both hands at chest
Resist rotation as bar wants to drop to one side
Slowly rotate from side to side with control
tips: Resist the rotation — that's the point

title: Leg Raise: Incline
primer: Incline leg raises increase the range of motion for abdominal and hip flexor work.
steps: Lie on incline bench with head up

title: Chest Fly: Standing Band
primer: Standing band flyes allow chest fly movement without cables or equipment.
steps: Bring arms together in front of chest

title: Glute Kickback: Machine
primer: Machine glute kickbacks provide consistent resistance for glute isolation.
steps: Position in machine with foot on pad

title: Hip Hinge: Single Leg
primer: Single leg hip hinges build hip stability and reinforce the hinge pattern.
steps: Hinge at hips extending back leg behind

title: Squat: Tempo
primer: Tempo squats maximize time under tension for muscle development.
steps: Drive back up at normal speed

title: Deadlift: Tempo
primer: Tempo deadlifts increase time under tension and improve technique at slow speeds.
steps: Take 3-4 seconds to lower bar to floor
Full lockout at top at normal speed
